title: WIRED Prime Day Deal Picks Under $100: Kindle, Fitbit, Earbuds
gist: Updated version of the WIRED under-$100 Prime Day deals guide (June 25 update), adding SwitchBot, NOCO, TP-Link, Yoto Player, and others. The SwitchBot fan is notable as SwitchBot's first fan product and supports Matter smart home standard via a hub bridge. The Yoto Player (screen-free audio player for kids) at $88 captures a 'no screen' parenting trend.

themes.theme: Smart Home Interoperability: Matter Standard Adoption

insights.claim: SwitchBot's first-ever fan product includes Matter compatibility via an add-on hub bridge ($120 extra), positioning SwitchBot—primarily known for robot vacuums—as a broader smart home Matter-compatible device company.
insights.speaker: Kat Merck
insights.so_what: Matter compatibility is becoming a table-stakes differentiator for smart home devices; SwitchBot's entry into non-vacuum categories using Matter signals broadening competitive dynamics in the smart home platform market.
insights.quote: rare Matter compatibility with the add-on SwitchBot Hub 3 bridge

insights.claim: Yoto Player—a screen-free audio device for children aged 3-12 using physical cards to play audio content—was included in WIRED's deals guide at $88 (20% off), reflecting growing editorial attention to 'no screen' child-tech products.
insights.speaker: Nena Farrell
insights.so_what: Screen-free kid tech emerging as a notable product category in major tech publications signals growing parental demand for digital-wellbeing alternatives—a market niche with potential for premium pricing and subscription content attach.
insights.quote: a gadget that makes a great gift for a kid, but doesn't give them another screen
